mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate employment income, which drives higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns following payroll growth in more than 50% of months, or rank correlation between PAYEMS change and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that tightens financial conditions and depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or the rank information coefficient over triggered periods is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based demand strength that amplifies corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns when both series rise over a 3-month window.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to outperform the weaker single-series signal by at least 2 percentage points in accuracy, or produces a rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a 0.3 percentage point or greater rise in unemployment over three months signals impending earnings downgrades and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of weeks when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% over three months signals weakening manufacturing profitability and broader economic deceleration, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO declines > 1.0% over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate output and corporate revenue growth, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation accelerating above a 3.0% annualized rate over three months compresses real earnings expectations and raises tightening risk, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL accelerates above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in retail sales and subdued consumer price inflation indicates healthy real demand that supports profit margins without triggering monetary contraction, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: nominal demand expansion with anchored inflation
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarters when RSAFS rises and CPIAUCSL 12-month change remains below 3.0%.
